headline: SILVER SWINGS TO $82 AS CEASEFIRE CRACKS — PRECIOUS METALS VOLATILE AMID GEOPOLITICAL FLUX
snippet: Gold and silver whipsawed as the 10-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire showed signs of strain, with silver having surged 5% to $82/oz on Friday before giving back gains. The metal still posted its fourth consecutive weekly gain and sits 30% above its March low, with structural supply deficits and industrial demand from solar, EVs, and AI data centers keeping bulls in control.

headline: MOTLEY FOOL: SHOULD YOU BUY USAR UNDER $20 AS OKLAHOMA MAGNET LINE GOES LIVE?
snippet: USA Rare Earth commissioned Phase 1a of its Stillwater, Oklahoma NdFeB sintered magnet production line, making it one of the only U.S.-based suppliers outside China. The Motley Fool weighs the investment case for USAR below $20, factoring in $3.1B in combined federal and private backing, a new distribution deal with Arnold Magnetic Technologies, and the company's integrated mine-to-magnet platform targeting 600 t/yr capacity by year-end.

headline: COPPER NEARS $13,000/TONNE IN LONDON ON TARIFF FEARS AND GLOBAL SUPPLY STRAIN
snippet: Copper surged near $13,000/tonne in London — hitting fresh highs — before plunging in New York as tariff uncertainty and geopolitical risk created a sharp two-market divergence. J.P. Morgan projects a refined copper shortfall of 330,000 metric tonnes in 2026, compounding structural tightness as Chile accelerates permitting and global smelter throughput tightens.

headline: B.C. EXPLORATION HITS RECORD $751M; COPPER OVERTAKES GOLD AS TOP TARGET FOR FIRST TIME EVER
snippet: British Columbia's mineral exploration spending hit a record C$751 million in 2025 — up 36% year-over-year — with copper accounting for C$384M and overtaking gold as the province's top target for the first time in survey history. The Golden Triangle absorbed C$436M of investment, and B.C. is now fast-tracking permit approvals to 40–140 days as of April 1, 2026.

headline: LITHIUM GIANT SQM CALLS THE BOTTOM AT $15-18/KG AS PRICES REBOUND 130% FROM 2025 LOWS
snippet: SQM Vice President Carlos Diaz is forecasting a $15–18/kg lithium carbonate price floor for 2026 as the market rebounds 130% from last year's trough to approximately $22,500/tonne. The recovery is driven by Zimbabwe's concentrate export suspension, Australian supply discipline from 2024's crash, and surging Chinese battery storage demand that Morgan Stanley projects will produce an 80,000-tonne LCE deficit by year-end.

headline: ENERGY FUELS RAISES 2026 URANIUM OUTLOOK; LOCKS IN NEW LONG-TERM U.S. NUCLEAR CONTRACTS TO 2032
snippet: Energy Fuels (NYSE: UUUU) raised its 2026 uranium sales guidance to 1.5–2.0 million pounds of U3O8 and announced two new long-term delivery contracts with U.S. nuclear power generators running through 2032. The stock initially rallied before pulling back as broader uranium equities cooled; the company plans to pivot White Mesa from uranium milling to commercial-scale heavy rare earth production in H2 2026.

headline: GOLD MINERS PRINTING RECORD CASH FLOWS BUT STILL TRADING AT 44% DISCOUNT TO SPOT NAV
snippet: Despite gold above $4,800/oz and major producers posting their fifth consecutive quarter of windfall profits, GDX-tracked miners still trade at a 44% discount to spot-price NAV and a 10% discount to historical EV/EBITDA multiples. U.S. metals and mining stocks appear undervalued by 16–37% on InvestingPro Fair Value models, suggesting the equity re-rating has barely begun.

headline: GOLDSEEK COT REPORT: SPECULATIVE GOLD AND SILVER POSITIONING AHEAD OF FRIDAY'S CEASEFIRE SURGE
snippet: GoldSeek's weekly Commitments of Traders analysis for the week ending April 14, 2026 breaks down speculative and commercial positioning across COMEX gold, silver, and the U.S. Dollar Index — essential context for gauging whether institutional longs have room to run or a positioning unwind is approaching after Friday's precious metals spike.

headline: MONEY METALS: HOW A SILVER SHORTAGE SPARKED A HISTORIC RALLY — AND WHY $82 IS JUST THE BEGINNING
snippet: Money Metals Exchange traces the structural anatomy of silver's bull run: six straight years of supply deficits, lease rates exceeding 200%, and China's new sulfuric acid export ban threatening the 70% of silver supply mined as a byproduct of copper operations. With Western retail investors only beginning to accumulate, the analysis argues that $82/oz is a midpoint in a longer repricing, not a ceiling.
